headline: Tech bulls triumph as NVDA soars while energy craters on peace hopes
summary: Models showed strong consensus on tech direction with NVDA calls proving particularly prescient, while energy sector XLE cratered to $55.95 from $82+ predictions. Despite bullish tech consensus, most models underestimated the actual moves with SPY closing at $731.58 and QQQ at $694.94, far exceeding even optimistic targets.

consensus_picks.note: All four models called SPY higher but only Gemini got it right as others set targets above actual close.
Tech ETF consensus was directionally right for Gemini only, with others setting targets too high despite correct direction.
All three models correctly called NVDA's rise with Grok's $212 target nearly perfect at $211.50 close.
Bears got crushed as TSLA rallied to $411.79, defying all three models predicting decline to ~$390-395.
Both models called for $290 but Apple fell short at $287.44, missing targets by over $2.

best_call.summary: Grok nailed NVDA's move with 70% confidence, citing options flow data showing heavy call buying that played out perfectly.

worst_call.summary: Claude's high-confidence QQQ call for $705 missed badly as the ETF closed at $694.94 despite correct directional call.
